claim_boundary: Tier 4.30-readiness is a local engineering audit. It does not implement lifecycle hardware, does not run EBRAINS/SpiNNaker, does not prove native lifecycle, does not migrate v2.2 temporal state, and does not freeze a new lifecycle or native baseline.

criteria.name: v2.2 baseline exists and is frozen
v2.2 boundary excludes hardware/on-chip temporal claim
value: v2.2 is host-side software evidence for bounded multi-timescale fading-memory temporal state.
v2.2 does not prove bounded nonlinear recurrence; Tier 5.19b/5.19c explicitly leave recurrence unproven.
v2.2 does not prove hardware transfer or native on-chip temporal dynamics.
v2.2 does not prove universal superiority on Mackey-Glass, Lorenz, NARMA10, or external baselines; lag-only remains stronger on the standard-three geomean in this gate.
v2.2 does not prove language, long-horizon planning, AGI, or ASI.

criteria.name: MCPL data plane available for native scaling path
legacy dynamic birth/death identified
criteria.note: Existing neuron_birth/death uses SDRAM allocation/free and must not be treated as the static-pool lifecycle proof.
criteria.rule: == true and excluded from initial lifecycle-native proof

criteria.name: lifecycle static-pool fields are not already implemented
criteria.note: This is expected at readiness stage; Tier 4.30 must add a bounded lifecycle state surface before hardware.

explicitly_out_of_scope: legacy SDRAM malloc/free neuron_birth/neuron_death as the lifecycle proof
initially_in_scope: compact readback and local fixed-point parity
layering_decision.why: Lifecycle/self-scaling is an organism/ecology mechanism. The existing native bridge already owns context, route, memory, prediction, confidence, replay, pending maturation, and MCPL-distributed lookup primitives. v2.2 adds useful host-side fading-memory temporal state, but it is not yet native/on-chip and should not be smuggled into the first lifecycle hardware gate.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: Lifecycle-enabled path must beat or differ from fixed active mask only on lifecycle-pressure tasks.
lifecycle_controls.name: fixed_static_pool_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Shows any benefit is not just the preallocated max capacity.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: Random matched events should not reproduce lineage/trophic/task benefit.
lifecycle_controls.name: random_event_replay_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Matches event count while destroying event causality.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: Mask shuffle should break lineage-specific or specialist reuse claims.
lifecycle_controls.name: active_mask_shuffle_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Destroys identity-to-slot binding while preserving active count.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: Lineage shuffle should damage lineage-dependent claims without changing raw capacity.
lifecycle_controls.name: lineage_id_shuffle_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Checks whether lineage bookkeeping is causal or decorative.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: No-trophic path should lose lifecycle selection-specific effects.
lifecycle_controls.name: no_trophic_pressure_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Removes ecology pressure while leaving state machinery alive.

lifecycle_controls.expected_effect: No learning/plasticity should not pass task-effect gates.
lifecycle_controls.name: no_dopamine_or_plasticity_control
lifecycle_controls.purpose: Separates lifecycle bookkeeping from actual learning/reward coupling.

recommended_sequence: Tier 4.30 contract: formalize static-pool lifecycle surface and command/readback schema.
Tier 4.30a local reference: deterministic static-pool event stream and fixed-point parity.
Tier 4.30b single-core hardware smoke: active-mask and lineage telemetry only.
Tier 4.30c multi-core lifecycle state split if 4.30b passes.
Tier 4.30d lifecycle sham-control hardware subset before any lifecycle-native baseline freeze.
Separate native temporal-readiness tier only if v2.2 fading-memory state is selected for hardware migration.
required_artifacts: local fixed-point reference JSON for Tier 4.30a
prepared EBRAINS source-only package only after local pass
ingested hardware report only after returned real hardware artifacts

runtime_observations.neuron_manager_boundary: neuron_birth/neuron_death are useful legacy primitives but use SDRAM allocation/free. Tier 4.30 lifecycle-native proof must use a static pool/mask/lineage surface instead of dynamic allocation.

static_pool_contract.capacity_rule: fixed compile-time pool; events toggle/assign slots rather than allocate/free graph objects

event_semantics.birth: activate one inactive slot after adult gates pass; parent remains active
event_semantics.cleavage: activate one inactive slot from a parent lineage with deterministic parent/child split
event_semantics.death: clear active_mask and preserve final lineage/event telemetry
event_semantics.handoff: record maturity transition; no dynamic graph creation

static_pool_contract.first_hardware_task: single-board seed-42 lifecycle mask smoke only after local parity and source audit
static_pool_contract.first_local_task: short static-pool lifecycle event stream with fixed reference and no hardware allocation
static_pool_contract.runtime_owner: new lifecycle state path or lifecycle profile layered beside existing state/context/route/memory/learning cores
